title: Strings, Byte Buffers, Length, Capacity, and Encoding · Memory Trace: String Buffer Distinction
summary: An accessible terminal application receives filenames, UTF-8 labels, and binary payloads through APIs that disagree about termination and length. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem string buffer distinction increment in length-aware text and byte boundary. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish C strings, unterminated character arrays, byte buffers, string literals, and encoded text.
Calculate multidimensional offsets with checked dimensions, row stride, element size, and layout evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset.
